items.title: EPSS Surge: CVE-2021-25297
items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2021-25297 surged from 43% to 59% (percentile 99%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2021-era vulnerability. It is not (yet) in CISA KEV — this signal typically precedes KEV listing and press coverage. Nagios XI version xi-5.7.5 is affected by OS command injection. The vulnerability exists in the file /usr/local/nagiosxi/html/includes/configwizards/switch/switch.inc.php due to improper sanitization of authenticated user-controlled input by a single HTTP request, which can lead to OS command injection on the Nagios XI server.
items.description: Nagios XI version xi-5.7.5 is affected by OS command injection. The vulnerability exists in the file /usr/local/nagiosxi/html/includes/configwizards/switch/switch.inc.php due to improper sanitization of authenticated user-controlled input by a single HTTP request, which can lead to OS command injection on the Nagios XI server.

priority.reason: EPSS 59% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 8.8.

impact.how_to_check: Identify systems running the software class described in the report and review vendor advisories for applicability.

items.tanium_hint: This device is typically a network appliance, not a Tanium-managed endpoint. If it isn't under Tanium, pivot to the Open Ports sensor on adjacent managed hosts to spot exposed management interfaces, and use your network/asset inventory to confirm firmware versions.
items.rapid7_hint: In InsightVM, find the vulnerability check matching the software in this report, confirm it's in your active scan template, and prefer a credentialed scan for reliable confirmation over a version-only match.
